history_completeness.note: Phase-1 writer starts from configured/current tail. Earlier history must be backfilled or supplied by verified IPFS backfill or a signed checkpoint before full bootstrap use.

ipns_publish_policy.dht_persistence_note: IPNS DHT records must be republished periodically; a long record lifetime is not a substitute for maintenance republish.
ipns_publish_policy.republish_interval: 5m plus up to 60s randomized delay when background maintenance is allowed
ipns_publish_policy.resolver_cache_note: Resolvers may briefly return older valid cached IPNS records. Freshness-sensitive consumers should use uncached resolution where available and verify the returned index CID against this discovery file and the signed segment index.

peer_roster_trust_model: Peer rosters are signed bootstrap hints only. Consumers must verify the roster signature before use, TCP-probe candidates, and still rely on live chain consensus for block validity.

provider.notes: Use IPFS provider discovery for immutable CIDs. The IPNS name is the durable moving latest pointer for future updates.

trust_model: IPFS and IPNS are byte-transport discovery hints only. Receivers must verify signed manifests, network, genesis, tip, state root, and normal consensus before using data.
